decisions.rationale: V<=1 (Cauchy-Schwarz) => delta<=0 => tail correction non-positive and small. Core positive by Chebyshev. Margin 4.4x at N=10 to 49x at N=500. Taylor expansion FAILS (h'' too large).

context_notes: Session 13 closed the main proof gap. The key insight: V(g) <= 1 is EXACT (from spectral variance identity), which forces h(g) <= sqrt(2/pi). This makes the decomposition h = h_core + delta clean: h_core is monotone (Chebyshev gives Cov>0), delta <= 0 (tail correction is provably non-positive and small). The proof is now at 'theorem + computation' level. Two papers: rigidity_exclusion_v3.tex (density bound) and r_positivity_theorem.tex (proof of r>0, NOW RIGOROUS). Venv still broken; use /c/Python313/python.exe.
